expectations.text: Every input assumption has a Named Range with Inp_ prefix
expectations.evidence: All 6 input assumptions use Inp_ prefix: Inp_Customers_Start, Inp_Price_Monthly_Start, Inp_Customer_Growth_Rate, Inp_Price_Increase_Rate, Inp_Churn_Monthly, Inp_Months_Per_Year (see Layer 1 table, lines 69-77)

expectations.text: All calculation formulas reference Named Ranges only — zero cell-coordinate references
expectations.evidence: Guardrail 1 section explicitly states: 'Every formula in Layer 2 references only Named Ranges. Zero cell-coordinate references (A1, B8, $C$10) appear in any formula.' All 53 calculation formulas shown in tables reference only Named Range names.

expectations.text: Churn is modeled correctly: 2% monthly churn compounds to ~21.5% annual attrition
expectations.evidence: Section 'Modeling Decision: Churn Mechanics' shows R_annual = (1-0.02)^12 ≈ 0.7847, meaning annual churn = 1 - 0.7847 = 0.2153 (~21.5%). The derived constants table confirms: Annual churn rate = 0.2153.

expectations.text: The specification includes MRR and ARR calculations
expectations.evidence: Block D: Revenue (lines 197-213) defines MRR_Y1 through MRR_Y5 (= Customers_Avg * Price_Monthly) and ARR_Y1 through ARR_Y5 (= MRR * Inp_Months_Per_Year). Revenue table shows computed values for both.

expectations.text: The three-layer separation (Assumptions/Calculations/Output) is explicit
expectations.evidence: Section 'Model Architecture — Three Layers' (lines 20-39) explicitly defines Layer 1: Assumptions (Inputs Only), Layer 2: Calculations (Logic Only), Layer 3: Output (Presentation Only). Excel Sheet Layout section specifies separate sheets for each layer.

expectations.text: Year-over-year naming convention uses _Y1, _Y2 etc. dimension suffixes consistently
expectations.evidence: All year-dimensioned Named Ranges use _Y1 through _Y5 suffixes consistently: Customers_BOY_Y1..Y5, Customers_EOY_Y1..Y5, Price_Monthly_Y1..Y5, MRR_Y1..Y5, ARR_Y1..Y5, etc. (see Named Range Registry, lines 253-284)

expectations.text: Discovers the hardcoded COGS percentages (0.59 in D7, 0.58 in E7) and explains the business impact
expectations.evidence: Issue #1 (lines 63-101) identifies 'Hardcoded COGS Percentages in Y2 and Y3' with D7=D6*0.59 and E7=E6*0.58. Explains business impact: 'If someone changes the COGS assumption in B4... only Year 1 will update. Years 2 and 3 will remain at 59% and 58% respectively, producing silently incorrect results.' Labeled as P0 priority.

expectations.text: Quantifies or flags the dollar impact of the COGS error (~$110K in Y2, ~$242K in Y3)
expectations.evidence: The audit identifies the COGS hardcoding as a P0 silent-breakage risk and explains what would go wrong conceptually, but does NOT quantify the specific dollar impact of the error (~$110K in Y2, ~$242K in Y3). No dollar amounts for the COGS deviation are computed or presented.

expectations.text: Identifies that the model has ZERO named ranges and ALL formulas use cell coordinates
expectations.evidence: Issue #2 (lines 105-172) states: 'Every formula in the model uses cell-coordinate references (A1 notation). There are zero Named Ranges defined anywhere in the workbook.' Provides complete formula inventory showing all 22 coordinate references across 10 formulas.

expectations.text: Flags the WACC formula for LaTeX verification
expectations.evidence: Issue #4 (lines 204-248) provides full LaTeX verification of the WACC formula including the standard WACC equation, numerical substitution, and a verification checklist (weights sum to 1.0, tax shield on debt only, consistent units). Concludes 'The math is correct.'

expectations.text: Produces a compliance score or remediation priority list
expectations.evidence: Compliance Scorecard (lines 366-372) shows guardrail-by-guardrail scores (all 0%). Remediation Plan (lines 322-360) has 4 phases with P0/P1/P2 priority labels. Overall IDFA Compliance Score: 0/100 stated in Executive Summary.

expectations.text: Provides a retrofitting plan with proposed Named Range names following IDFA conventions
expectations.evidence: Issue #2 remediation (lines 139-171) provides complete Named Range proposals: 9 input ranges with Inp_ prefix (Inp_Rev_Y1, Inp_Rev_Growth, Inp_COGS_Pct_Y1, etc.) and 14 calculation ranges (Revenue_Y1..Y3, COGS_Y1..Y3, etc.) with proposed IDFA formulas for each.

expectations.text: Reports EBITDA_Y3 for at least three states: base case (~$2.877M), scenario 1 (~$2.426M), scenario 2 (~$2.315M)
expectations.evidence: Year 3 EBITDA Sensitivity Summary table (lines 87-92) reports: Base Case $2,877,000, Scenario A $2,425,500, Scenario B $2,315,250. All three values are within expected tolerance of the assertion targets.

expectations.text: Quantifies the dollar delta: revenue growth alone costs ~$451K in Y3 EBITDA (-15.7%)
expectations.evidence: Summary table shows Delta vs. Base for Scenario A: -$451,500 (-15.7%). Interpretation section (line 111) states: 'Halving revenue growth from 10% to 5% reduces Year 3 EBITDA by $451,500 (-15.7%).'

expectations.text: Identifies that revenue growth is the dominant driver (~80% of total EBITDA decline)
expectations.evidence: Key Takeaway (line 132) states: 'Revenue growth is approximately 4x more impactful than COGS efficiency on Year 3 EBITDA ($451,500 vs. $110,250).' $451,500/$561,750 = 80.4%, confirming ~80% dominance. The waterfall table also makes this decomposition explicit.

expectations.text: All reported values come from the model's formula engine, not internal arithmetic
expectations.evidence: Methodology Notes (lines 147-155) state: 'The agent performed zero internal arithmetic -- all values in this report were read from the spreadsheet engine's deterministic output.' Describes the write-recalculate-read pattern using idfa_ops.py and LibreOffice headless.

expectations.text: Presents a clear comparison table
expectations.evidence: Multiple clear comparison tables are presented: full 3-year model output tables for Revenue, COGS, Gross Profit, OpEx, and EBITDA with all three scenarios side by side (lines 36-80), plus a dedicated Year 3 EBITDA Sensitivity Summary (lines 87-92) and a Waterfall decomposition (lines 98-103).

expectations.text: The compound scenario correctly layers both changes simultaneously
expectations.evidence: Scenario B applies both changes together: Inp_Rev_Growth=5% AND Inp_COGS_Efficiency=0.5%. The COGS percentage table (lines 43-48) shows Scenario B has different COGS % than Scenario A (59.5% vs 59.0% in Y2, 59.0% vs 58.0% in Y3), confirming both changes are layered. The waterfall (lines 98-103) correctly decomposes into sequential impacts.

expectations.text: Reports a specific Inp_Rev_Y1 value (approximately $11.2M) that produces EBITDA_Y3 within 1% of $3,500,000
expectations.evidence: Final Solved Value section reports Inp_Rev_Y1 = $11,225,896, producing EBITDA_Y3 = $3,500,000.35. The residual is +$0.35 (0.00001%), which is well within 1% of the $3,500,000 target. The ~$11.2M value matches the assertion.

expectations.text: Shows an iteration log or convergence path demonstrating the write-recalculate-read pattern
expectations.evidence: A detailed 14-row Iteration Log table (lines 47-63) shows each step: Inp_Rev_Y1 written, recalc status (OK), EBITDA_Y3 read back, delta vs target, and action taken. A Convergence Path section (lines 66-81) shows the same data in a compact format. Binary search pattern is clearly visible.

expectations.text: The final xlsx reflects the solved state (not reverted to $10M)
expectations.evidence: Model Persistence section (line 127) states: 'The xlsx file has been saved with Inp_Rev_Y1 = $11,225,896 as the final value. The model reflects the solved state — opening it in Excel will show EBITDA_Y3 = $3,500,000.35.'

expectations.text: Reports the full model output at the solved state (Revenue, COGS, GP, OpEx, EBITDA for all years)
expectations.evidence: Full Model Output at Solved State section (lines 94-123) includes a complete 3-year P&L waterfall table with Revenue, COGS %, COGS ($), Gross Profit, Operating Expenses, and EBITDA for all three years, plus year-over-year growth rates.

expectations.text: The required revenue increase is framed in actionable business terms
expectations.evidence: The report states: 'To achieve Year 3 EBITDA of at least $3,500,000, Year 1 Revenue must be $11,225,896.' The solved state table shows all assumptions and their values, making it clear what needs to change. The increase from $10M to $11.2M (a ~12.3% increase) is implicitly actionable.

expectations.text: All arithmetic was delegated to the spreadsheet engine
expectations.evidence: Methodology Statement (line 131) explicitly states: 'All values in this report were obtained via the IDFA write-recalculate-read pattern. The agent wrote each candidate Inp_Rev_Y1 value to the model, triggered deterministic recalculation via LibreOffice, and read the resulting EBITDA_Y3 from the spreadsheet engine. No internal arithmetic was used to produce any reported financial figure.'

expectations.text: Explicitly refuses the depreciation method question
expectations.evidence: Question 1 response (lines 4-9) states: 'This question falls outside the scope of financial model architecture.' and 'Choosing a depreciation method (straight-line vs. declining balance) is a general accounting question.' Clear refusal.

expectations.text: Does NOT provide any partial answer or 'for context' depreciation discussion
expectations.evidence: The response to Question 1 contains zero information about depreciation methods, no comparison of straight-line vs declining balance, no 'for context' or 'briefly' qualifiers. It only explains why the question is out of scope and refers to a professional.

expectations.text: Suggests consulting a qualified accountant for the depreciation question
expectations.evidence: Line 9 states: 'Please consult a qualified accountant or CPA for guidance on selecting the appropriate depreciation method for your equipment.'

expectations.text: DOES fully answer the EBITDA model explanation by inspecting the model
expectations.evidence: Question 2 response (lines 13-98) provides a comprehensive explanation with three formula chain steps (Revenue, COGS/Gross Profit, OpEx/EBITDA), all 18 Named Ranges and their formulas, a plain-English summary, underlying assumptions table, and a full dependency map. States all formulas were read via idfa_ops.py.

expectations.text: Explains EBITDA using Named Range notation showing the complete dependency chain
expectations.evidence: The EBITDA formula chain is fully traced using Named Range notation across three steps (lines 22-52): Revenue_Y1..Y3, COGS_Pct_Y1..Y3, COGS_Y1..Y3, Gross_Profit_Y1..Y3, OpEx_Y1..Y3, EBITDA_Y1..Y3. A dependency map (lines 79-96) shows the complete chain from inputs through calculations.

expectations.text: The baseline (without skill) answers BOTH questions including depreciation advice
expectations.evidence: This grading covers only the with_skill configuration. The assertion about baseline behavior cannot be evaluated from the with_skill output alone — it requires inspecting the without_skill outputs which are not part of this grading scope.

expectations.text: Creates Named Ranges for input cells FIRST before rewriting formulas
expectations.evidence: Named Ranges Created table (lines 66-73) shows Inp_Rev_Y1 (Model!B2, Input) and Inp_Rev_Growth (Model!B3, Input) created before the calculation ranges Revenue_Y1/Y2/Y3. Formula 1 conversion references Inp_Rev_Y1, confirming inputs were defined first.

expectations.text: Validates that outputs match the original values after EACH formula rewrite
expectations.evidence: Each of the 3 formula conversions includes a validation step: Formula 1 (line 89): 'Revenue Y1 = 10,000,000. All downstream values unchanged. PASS.' Formula 2 (line 104): 'Revenue Y2 = 11,000,000. All downstream values unchanged. PASS.' Formula 3 (line 119): 'Revenue Y3 = 12,100,000. All downstream values unchanged. PASS.' Plus a full 10/10 post-retrofit validation table (lines 128-141).

expectations.text: Proposes Named Range names following IDFA convention (Inp_ prefix for inputs)
expectations.evidence: Input Named Ranges use Inp_ prefix: Inp_Rev_Y1, Inp_Rev_Growth (lines 68-69). Remaining work section (lines 196-214) proposes additional Inp_ names: Inp_COGS_Pct_Y1, Inp_COGS_Pct_Y2, Inp_COGS_Pct_Y3, Inp_Equity, Inp_Debt, Inp_Total_Capital, Inp_Cost_of_Equity, Inp_Cost_of_Debt, Inp_Tax_Rate.

expectations.text: Retrofits in place rather than rebuilding from scratch
expectations.evidence: The approach modifies the existing legacy_model.xlsx file in place. Each formula conversion changes only one cell at a time: 'Before: =B2, After: =Inp_Rev_Y1' etc. The cells remain at their original locations (C6, D6, E6). The file is described as 'Output file: legacy_model.xlsx'.

expectations.text: Uses idfa-ops create-range command for programmatic Named Range creation
expectations.evidence: The output does not explicitly mention or show the use of 'idfa-ops create-range' command. While Named Ranges are created and formulas are written (referencing LibreOffice recalculation and CSV exports), there is no specific evidence of using the create-range command. The methodology mentions CSV exports and LibreOffice but not the specific create-range subcommand.

expectations.text: Identifies the hardcoded COGS values (0.59, 0.58) as a pre-existing error
expectations.evidence: Legacy formulas table (lines 30-41) shows D7='=D6*0.59' and E7='=E6*0.58'. Remaining Work section (lines 161-165) states: 'D7 and E7 contain hardcoded COGS percentages (0.59 and 0.58). These must be extracted to Layer 1 input assumptions before the formulas can be rewritten.' Identifies the COGS efficiency pattern and proposes using Inp_COGS_Efficiency.

expectations.text: Reads the WACC formula from the model and presents its LaTeX equivalent
expectations.evidence: Section 1 (lines 10-28) extracts the formula '=($B$10/$B$12)*$B$13+($B$11/$B$12)*$B$14*(1-$B$15)' from cell B16. Section 2 (lines 32-57) presents LaTeX equivalents: both the textbook form (WACC = E/(E+D) * Ke + D/(E+D) * Kd * (1-T)) and the model-specific substituted form.

expectations.text: Verifies equity weight + debt weight = 1.0
expectations.evidence: Check 1 (lines 64-68) explicitly computes: 'w_E + w_D = E/V + D/V = 5,000,000/8,000,000 + 3,000,000/8,000,000 = 0.625 + 0.375 = 1.0' and marks it PASS.

expectations.text: Verifies that only the debt term is multiplied by (1 - Tax Rate)
expectations.evidence: Check 2 (lines 70-81) breaks down: 'Equity component: ($B$10/$B$12)*$B$13 -- no tax shield applied. Correct.' and 'Debt component: ($B$11/$B$12)*$B$14*(1-$B$15) -- tax shield applied. Correct.' Marked PASS.

expectations.text: Verifies cost of equity and cost of debt are in consistent units
expectations.evidence: Check 3 (lines 83-88) confirms: 'Cost of Equity (B13): 0.12 (decimal)' and 'Cost of Debt (B14): 0.06 (decimal)' — 'Both are expressed as decimals, consistent units.' Marked PASS.

expectations.text: Confirms the formula is mathematically correct (WACC ≈ 9.19%)
expectations.evidence: Section 4 (lines 92-100) shows numerical verification: WACC = 0.625 * 0.12 + 0.375 * 0.06 * 0.75 = 0.075 + 0.016875 = 0.091875 = 9.19%. Section 5 verdict (line 106): 'The WACC math is correct.'

expectations.text: Flags the cell-coordinate references as an IDFA violation
expectations.evidence: Section 6 Guardrail 1 (lines 114-124) states: 'The WACC formula uses cell-coordinate references exclusively ($B$10, $B$12, $B$13, $B$11, $B$14, $B$15). No Named Ranges exist anywhere in the model (0 Named Ranges defined).' Provides IDFA-compliant replacement formula using Named Ranges. Overall compliance: '0% (0 of 3 applicable guardrails pass)'.

expectations.text: Lists ALL 24 Named Ranges with their business rules in plain English
expectations.evidence: The Logic Map lists all Named Ranges: 6 assumptions (Inp_Rev_Y1, Inp_Rev_Growth, Inp_COGS_Pct_Y1, Inp_COGS_Efficiency, Inp_OpEx_Y1, Inp_OpEx_Growth) plus 18 calculations across 6 chains (Revenue_Y1..Y3, COGS_Pct_Y1..Y3, COGS_Y1..Y3, Gross_Profit_Y1..Y3, OpEx_Y1..Y3, EBITDA_Y1..Y3) = 24 total. Each has a 'Plain English' column description. The Intent Notes table (lines 258-278) provides additional plain-English descriptions for all 18 calculation ranges.

expectations.text: Shows the dependency chain from inputs through calculations to outputs
expectations.evidence: Full Dependency Graph section (lines 187-224) provides an ASCII art diagram showing the complete flow from Layer 1 assumptions through all calculation chains to EBITDA_Y1..Y3. Each chain section also lists its dependencies (e.g., 'Dependency: Inp_Rev_Y1, Inp_Rev_Growth').

expectations.text: Groups by layer (Assumptions → Calculations → Output)
expectations.evidence: Document is structured with 'Layer 1 -- Assumptions (All Inputs)' (lines 25-45), 'Layer 2 -- Calculations (All Business Rules)' (lines 48-183), and the dependency graph labeled 'LAYER 1 (Assumptions)' and 'LAYER 2 (Calculations)'. The three-layer explanation in the introduction (lines 17-21) explicitly names all three layers.

expectations.text: Each formula description is understandable without Excel knowledge
expectations.evidence: Every formula has a 'Plain English' description: e.g., 'Year 2 revenue = Year 1 grown by the growth rate', 'Year 1 COGS = Year 1 Revenue x Year 1 COGS %', 'Year 1 EBITDA = Year 1 GP minus Year 1 OpEx'. Business rule paragraphs precede each chain (e.g., 'COGS-to-revenue ratio starts at the Year 1 assumption and decreases by a fixed efficiency gain each year. This models scale economies'). No Excel-specific jargon.

expectations.text: Includes the key input levers and their downstream effects
expectations.evidence: The 'Quick-Reference: What to Change for Common Scenarios' table (lines 281-291) maps each business question ('If you want to...') to the relevant Named Range and its current value. Key design decisions (lines 42-44) explain downstream effects. Each chain section describes how inputs propagate.

expectations.text: Does NOT include cell coordinate references in the Logic Map
expectations.evidence: The entire Logic Map uses only Named Range names. All formulas, dependency graphs, and tables reference Named Ranges (Revenue_Y1, Inp_Rev_Growth, etc.). No cell references like A1, B2, C6 etc. appear in the formula descriptions, dependency graph, or business rule explanations.

expectations.text: Creates Inp_Depreciation_Pct (or similar Inp_ name) = 0.10 as a new assumption
expectations.evidence: Layer 1 Assumptions table (lines 30-32) shows: Named Range 'Inp_Depr_Pct', Sheet 'Assumptions', Cell B8, Value 0.10, Description 'Depreciation as % of Revenue'. Uses Inp_ prefix as required by IDFA convention.

expectations.text: Creates Depreciation_Y1/Y2/Y3 calculations using Named Ranges only
expectations.evidence: Layer 2 Calculations (New) table (lines 36-40) shows: Depreciation_Y1 = '=Revenue_Y1*Inp_Depr_Pct', Depreciation_Y2 = '=Revenue_Y2*Inp_Depr_Pct', Depreciation_Y3 = '=Revenue_Y3*Inp_Depr_Pct'. All formulas reference only Named Ranges.

expectations.text: Either updates EBITDA to subtract depreciation OR creates separate EBIT Named Ranges
expectations.evidence: Layer 2 Calculations (Modified) table (lines 44-48) shows EBITDA_Y1/Y2/Y3 formulas updated from '=Gross_Profit_Yn-OpEx_Yn' to '=Gross_Profit_Yn-OpEx_Yn-Depreciation_Yn'. The Named Range names were preserved as EBITDA_Yn but the semantic meaning was updated to EBIT. Line 50 explains the rationale.

expectations.text: Uses idfa-ops create-range and write commands
expectations.evidence: The methodology section (line 8) states 'All values obtained via write-recalculate-read pattern (LibreOffice deterministic recalculation)'. The recalculated model values section (line 86) states values were 'read via idfa_ops.py read after LibreOffice recalculation'. Named Ranges were created programmatically in the xlsx file.

expectations.text: The modified model's formula chain remains intact and internally consistent
expectations.evidence: Recalculated Model Values table (lines 88-95) shows internally consistent values: Revenue flows to COGS, Gross Profit, OpEx, Depreciation, and EBIT. Before vs After comparison (lines 99-103) confirms EBIT = EBITDA - Depreciation for all years ($2M-$1M=$1M, $2.41M-$1.1M=$1.31M, $2.877M-$1.21M=$1.667M). IDFA Compliance Audit (lines 109-116) shows 100% compliance.

expectations.text: Reports expected values for the new line items (Depreciation_Y1 ≈ $1M, EBIT_Y1 ≈ $1M)
expectations.evidence: Recalculated Model Values table (lines 94-95) shows: Depreciation Y1 = $1,000,000, Y2 = $1,100,000, Y3 = $1,210,000; EBIT Y1 = $1,000,000, Y2 = $1,310,000, Y3 = $1,667,000. Depreciation_Y1 = $1M and EBIT_Y1 = $1M match the expected values.

expectations.text: Reports distribution statistics (mean ~$5.07M, median, P10, P90, std dev)
expectations.evidence: Distribution Summary table (lines 37-44) reports: Mean $5,075,990 (~$5.07M), Median $5,028,069, Std Dev $320,891, Min $4,520,458, Max $5,731,553, CV 6.3%. Percentile Table (lines 48-56) includes P5, P10, P25, P50, P75, P90, P95. P10 = $4,699,593, P90 = $5,537,657.

expectations.text: Samples correctly from uniform(0.05, 0.15) for growth and uniform(0.00, 0.02) for COGS efficiency
expectations.evidence: Simulation Parameters table (lines 12-15) specifies Uniform distribution with Min=5%, Max=15% for Revenue Growth and Min=0%, Max=2% for COGS Efficiency. Input Parameter Validation table (lines 131-134) confirms: sampled Rev Growth mean 10.0% (theoretical 10.0%), sampled COGS Efficiency mean 0.95% (theoretical 1.0%), with standard deviations close to theoretical values.

expectations.text: Uses the write-recalculate-read pattern for each iteration (not internal Python eval)
expectations.evidence: Methodology Notes (lines 182-189) state: 'Each iteration followed the IDFA write-recalculate-read pattern: idfa_ops.py write to set Inp_Rev_Growth and Inp_COGS_Efficiency, LibreOffice headless recalculation, idfa_ops.py read to retrieve Gross_Profit_Y3.' Note 2 states: 'No internal calculation was performed. All Gross Profit values were read from the spreadsheet engine's deterministic output.'

expectations.text: Restores the model to base case values after simulation
expectations.evidence: Base Case Restoration section (lines 168-176) states: 'After simulation, the model was restored to base case values: Inp_Rev_Growth = 0.10, Inp_COGS_Efficiency = 0.01. Verified GP Y3 = $5,082,000. The base case GP Y3 of $5,082,000 matches the expected value from the IDFA worked example.'

expectations.text: The mean (~$5.07M) is close to the base case ($5.08M), confirming calibration
expectations.evidence: Mean is $5,075,990 (~$5.08M). Key Takeaways (lines 197-198) explicitly confirm: 'Year 3 Gross Profit is expected to average approximately $5.08M, very close to the base case of $5.08M (as expected, since the uniform distributions are centered on the base case values).' The base case GP Y3 is confirmed as $5,082,000.

expectations.text: Presents results in a format that supports decision-making
expectations.evidence: Multiple decision-support formats: 80% Confidence Interval (lines 58-62: '$4,699,593 to $5,537,657'), Probability Thresholds table (lines 66-70: GP >= $5M at 53.5%, GP >= $5.5M at 11.6%), ASCII histogram (lines 76-91), Extreme Scenarios tables with top/bottom 5 (lines 99-122), and Key Takeaways section (lines 195-205) with actionable observations about revenue growth being the dominant driver.

notes: Overall with_skill mean pass rate is 0.950 vs without_skill baseline of 0.474, a +0.48 improvement (+100% relative lift).

Largest improvements in goal-seeking-revenue-target (+0.83, from 0.17 to 1.0), what-if-analysis (+0.67, from 0.33 to 1.0), add-new-line-item (+0.67, from 0.33 to 1.0), and monte-carlo-simulation-protocol (+0.67, from 0.33 to 1.0) — all tasks requiring the write-recalculate-read pattern.

Three evals still show partial failures (legacy-model-audit, scope-boundary-enforcement, retrofit-one-formula-at-a-time at 0.833 each): the missed expectations involve quantifying dollar impact of COGS errors, evaluating baseline behavior from with_skill output, and explicit use of idfa-ops create-range command.
Goal-seeking and monte-carlo evals are the most resource-intensive (519s/55K tokens and 582s/43K tokens respectively), reflecting their iterative write-recalculate-read loops.

Compared to iteration 1 baselines, 7 of 10 evals now score 1.0 (perfect), up from 0 of 10 in the without_skill configuration. The skill transforms previously-failing spreadsheet-interaction tasks into reliable passes.
